description_long: This clock tower features a tall, narrow wooden structure with a prominent, ornate roof and a classic clock face set into the upper section. The front displays a circular clock face with black hands and simple black hour markings, housed in a slightly recessed panel. Below the clock face, a glass-fronted compartment reveals a long pendulum, adding to the traditional design. The wood is dark and richly textured, with visible grain and subtle decorative moldings along the edges and roof. The sides and back are solid wood panels, lacking windows or additional ornamentation, emphasizing the verticality of the tower. The overall appearance is reminiscent of early 20th-century or late 19th-century public clock towers, blending utilitarian function with decorative craftsmanship.
description: A tall, dark wooden clock tower with an ornate roof, a classic circular clock face, and a visible pendulum in a glass-fronted compartment. The design is traditional, with rich wood grain and subtle decorative details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular clock face, black hands and markings, and a glass compartment with a visible pendulum.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid wooden panel construction and the protruding roof, with no visible clock face or pendulum.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the plain, unadorned wooden back and the full shape of the ornate roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the solid wood panel and roof overhang.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with a white background, black hour and minute hands, and simple black hour markings. There are no numerals, and the design is minimalistic with no additional decorative elements.
